page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 64-bit architecture, utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing reaching 97017.01 and Geekbench software compilation with Clang scoring 42663.0. Cached read-only memory bandwidth is also strong at 3782.81 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a relative tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scoring 975.0 and single-threaded gzip compression rated as weak. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput at 44.93 GB/sec, demonstrate the efficiency of the 64 dedicated cores.

This instance is highly cost-efficient for scale-out, multi-threaded workloads that benefit from high network throughput and dedicated ARM cores without requiring local SSD storage. It is well-suited for high-performance computing, network appliances, static web serving, and distributed database operations, as evidenced by its strong Redis SET performance of 72.72 million operations per second. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or large-model LLM text generation due to lower single-core metrics.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated ARMv8 physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor at 2.5 GHz. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 128.0 GB of memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 97017.01 and static web serving throughput of 44.93 GB/sec, though single-core performance remains a relative tradeoff. This instance is highly cost-efficient for scale-out, network-heavy workloads, such as distributed databases, static web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation, while being less optimal for single-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for multi-threaded and network-heavy workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering 64 dedicated cores and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput networking
Strong multi-core performance and high cached memory read bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and network appliances
